title: Familiar letters
subtitle: Letters А К М О С Т

hook.text: You already know more Russian letters than you think. Six letters in the Cyrillic alphabet look almost identical to their Latin counterparts and make the same sounds. By the end of this lesson, you'll be reading real Russian words.

why.text: Starting with familiar letters removes the fear of a 'foreign' alphabet. Once you see that А, К, М, О, С, and Т work exactly like in English, the rest of the alphabet becomes a manageable set of new shapes to learn — not an alien system.

cards.title: Six letters you already know
cards.body: These six Cyrillic letters look and sound like their English equivalents: А (a), К (k), М (m), О (o), С (s), Т (t). When you see them in a Russian word, pronounce them exactly as you would in English.

cards.title: Reading your first words
cards.body: With just these six letters, you can already read real Russian words. Try sounding each one out letter by letter.

questions.prompt: What does the Russian word 'кот' mean?
questions.explanation: кот (kot) means 'cat'. К=k, О=o, Т=t — all familiar letters.

questions.prompt: Write the Russian word for 'juice' using these letters: А К М О С Т
questions.explanation: Juice is сок (sok) — С=s, О=o, К=k.

cards.title: Uppercase and lowercase
cards.body: Like English, Russian has uppercase and lowercase forms. For these six letters, they look the same as English: А/а, К/к, М/м, О/о, С/с, Т/т. Russian uses uppercase at the start of sentences and for proper nouns, just like English.

cards.title: Stressed vs unstressed О
cards.body: When О is stressed (emphasised), it sounds like 'o' in 'bore'. When unstressed, it reduces to an 'ah' sound. This is a key Russian pronunciation rule you'll use constantly. For now, just know it exists — we'll drill it in lesson 1-7.

examples.meaning: The first О is unstressed (sounds like 'ah')

questions.prompt: What letter does this look like in English? К
questions.explanation: К in Russian is the same as K in English — same shape, same sound.

questions.prompt: Read this word and type its meaning: мак
Read this word and type its meaning: так
questions.explanation: так (tak) means 'so' or 'like that'. Т=t, А=a, К=k.

questions.prompt: Which Russian word means 'volume' (of a book)?
questions.explanation: том (tom) means a volume or tome.
С always makes the 's' sound in Russian, just like in English.

questions.prompt: Read this word and type its meaning: сок

rules: А, К, М, О, С, Т look and sound the same as their English equivalents
Stressed О sounds like 'o' in 'bore'; unstressed О sounds like 'ah'
Russian uses uppercase for sentence starts and proper nouns, just like English
summary.nextLesson: Next: six letters that look like English but make completely different sounds — the 'false friends'.
